since_last: Since premarket: NVDA reclaimed $200, MU is defending $900, and the semi fade followed through — SMH −1.8% with all 13 watchlist names red.

cards.headline: CPI prints hot at 4.2%; stocks sell off, bonds stay calm
cards.body: May CPI headline hit 4.2% and the first reaction was risk-off, though the Dow cut its losses after midday comments on the Iran standoff (IBD, Jun 10). Midday tape: SPY −0.66%, QQQ −1.05%, TLT flat — equities are pricing inflation and geopolitical risk, bonds aren't flinching.

cards.headline: Chip selloff, day two: all 13 watchlist names red
cards.body: SMH −1.83% and SOXX −2.05% at midday with zero green on the semis board — QCOM −5.75%, AVGO −3.86%, ARM −3.61%, AMD −3.44% lead the downside (12:23 ET, ~15-min delayed). Second straight distribution day in a group still sitting on big YTD gains.
cards.watch: TSM relative strength vs SMH into 14:00 ET — cleanest tell on whether semi sellers are exhausting.

cards.headline: NVDA reclaims $200 — premarket fade neutralized, for now
cards.body: Premarket pointed to an open below $200; at midday it's $202.69 (−1.81%), back above the level and just under Tuesday's $203.24 close. The structure question moves to the close.
cards.watch: Holding $200 into the close keeps the reclaim valid; a late slip back under leaves Friday's gap-down structure in control.

cards.headline: Asia memory takes the hit; US complex absorbs it
cards.body: Overnight memory damage was Asian — SK hynix −7.5%, Samsung −6.1%, Kioxia −7.8%, Nanya −8.3% (local Jun 10 sessions) dragged the DRAM ETF −2.41%. US side holding up: MU −1.91% at $902.00 defending $900, SanDisk the complex's only green at +1.8%. Micron also tapped Bechtel for its New York fab (InvestorsHub, Jun 10).
cards.watch: A MU close above $900 keeps dip-buyers in control of the memory theme.

cards.headline: Good news keeps getting sold: TSM and MRVL
cards.body: TSM posted May sales +30% y/y and trades −2.60% at $413.93; Marvell drew public Jensen Huang praise on custom silicon (Motley Fool, Jun 10) and sits −2.98%. Two fresh positive catalysts, two red names — positioning talking, not fundamentals.

cards.headline: Risk radar: Oracle earnings tonight test the AI-capex tape
cards.body: Oracle reports Q4 after the close, already framed as the AI trade's next test (Yahoo Finance, Jun 10). On a second de-risking day with Iran headlines live, a soft capex print lands on a tape with no cushion — a strong one is the bounce catalyst. Binary event: intraday levels carry less weight until it's absorbed.

disclaimer: Key Takeaways are generated for informational and educational purposes only and do not constitute investment advice, a recommendation, or an offer to buy or sell any security. Markets move fast — data may be delayed and levels can change. Always do your own research.
sources: Yahoo Finance delayed quotes via the Santro AI pipeline; site news feed (IBD, Motley Fool, WSJ, InvestorsHub, GuruFocus, Zacks, Yahoo Finance); previous cycle = premarket block 7:00 AM ET.
